story_graph.protagonist_want: keep the name on the register
story_graph.reader_care_reason: the child is the only one fighting the erasure

nodes.typed_hook: The class clock runs backward during attendance.
nodes.typed_narration: The child grips the desk and raises a hand when the teacher skips the name.
nodes.next_page_question: Why does the clock move only at that name?
nodes.reader_retention_goal: Make the reader track the skipped name.
nodes.visual_memory_point: a pale register line under a backward clock

nodes.typed_hook: The name fades from the workbook.
nodes.typed_narration: The child rewrites the name, but the pencil mark turns to dust.
nodes.next_page_question: What happens if the name disappears before the bell?
nodes.final_hook_sentence: The dust gathers beneath the clock.
nodes.visual_memory_point: a workbook name turning into dust
nodes.threat_delta: The anomaly moves from clock to name.
nodes.reader_care_reason: the child tries to fix it
nodes.concrete_behavior: the child presses the pencil hard

repeated_clues.escalation: the clock clue now affects written identity

nodes.typed_hook: The register writes the name backward.
nodes.typed_narration: The child opens the register and sees the classroom clock drawn beside the blank line.
nodes.final_hook_sentence: The line fills with the child's name backward.
nodes.visual_memory_point: a register writing a name backward
nodes.opening_callback: The backward clock from the opening returns in the register.
nodes.twist_reveal: The clock was erasing the name from the beginning.
nodes.reader_care_reason: the child's action triggers the callback
